result:
I would make every human mind incapable of sustaining unchallenged hatred.  
Not by erasing disagreement or pain, but by wiring the brain so that the moment contempt hardens into the wish to erase another person, the mind is flooded with the full, unfiltered emotional life of the one it’s trying to annihilate. You would feel their childhood terror, their private joys, the way their mother’s voice sounded when they were sick—everything that makes them as irrevocably real as you are. The circuitry of cruelty would short-circuit on contact with that shared nakedness.  

No utopia, no forced harmony—just a single, inescapable mirror. The world would still argue, compete, grieve, but genocide, pogroms, lynchings, drone strikes on weddings, casual punches thrown in a bar—all of it would become neurologically impossible. We’d keep our complexity, lose our capacity to turn other people into shadows.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
